trees.street_address: Silas T Burgess Arboretum; 720 Moore Road, King of Prussia, Pennsylvania 19406
trees.display_address: Silas T Burgess Arboretum; 720 Moore Road, King of Prussia, Pennsylvania 19406
trees.alt_text: American Sycamore in Montgomery County, PA — Listed 100 ft tall, 16' 7" around
trees.pa_nominator: Upper Merion Township Shade Tree Commission
trees.pa_measure_crew: Jason Lubar, Julianne Schieffer, Barley Van Clief, Adi Shiloh
trees.pa_comments: Tree Tag #78. The Silas T Burgess Arboretum is on property owned by Upper Merion Township. The Arboretum and John Moore home (Moore-Irwin House) sit approximately ¼ mile outside Valley Forge National Historical Park. The home and property were once owned by John Moore who along with his brother and father owned and donated significant acreage for use by General Washington and his troops during the Revolutionary War. Moore Park in King of Prussia was named after the Moore Family. The Moore House itself was Headquarters for General Peter Muhlenberg during the winter encampment. Former Penn Del ISA Chapter President and Arborist Silas Burgess recognized the unique and valuable trees back in the 1950’s and soon began a personal effort to resurrect this rare public treasure on his own. Quickly it became a community effort and thousands of local citizens, schools, students were able to enjoy for decades. Efforts to once again restore the Arboretum are currently underway.

trees.street_address: Silas T Burgess Arboretum; 720 Moore Road, King of Prussia, Pennsylvania 19406
trees.display_address: Silas T Burgess Arboretum; 720 Moore Road, King of Prussia, Pennsylvania 19406
trees.alt_text: American Sycamore in Montgomery County, PA — Listed 90 ft tall, 16' 1" around
trees.pa_nominator: Upper Merion Township Shade Tree Commission
trees.pa_measure_crew: Jason Lubar, Julianne Schieffer, Barley Van Clief, Adi Shiloh
trees.pa_comments: Tree Tag #72. The Silas T Burgess Arboretum is on property owned by Upper Merion Township. The Arboretum and John Moore home (Moore-Irwin House) sit approximately ¼ mile outside Valley Forge National Historical Park. The home and property were once owned by John Moore who along with his brother and father owned and donated significant acreage for use by General Washington and his troops during the Revolutionary War. Moore Park in King of Prussia was named after the Moore Family. The Moore House itself was Headquarters for General Peter Muhlenberg during the winter encampment. Former Penn Del ISA Chapter President and Arborist Silas Burgess recognized the unique and valuable trees back in the 1950’s and soon began a personal effort to resurrect this rare public treasure on his own. Quickly it became a community effort and thousands of local citizens, schools, students were able to enjoy for decades. Efforts to once again restore the Arboretum are currently underway.

trees.pa_comments: Labeled as a Sugar Maple (Acer saccharum), but it is a Black Maple (Acer nigrum, which was formerly called Acer saccharum ssp. nigrum).  It has the typical leaf shape of Black Maple, pubescence on the lower leaf surface, and a stipule scar at the base of the petiole.  Its fruit, tree shape, including the large horizontal branches, fall leaf color, and bark match those of Black Maple.

trees.pa_comments: Tree Tag #1. The Silas T Burgess Arboretum is on property owned by Upper Merion Township. The Arboretum and John Moore home (Moore-Irwin House) sit approximately ¼ mile outside Valley Forge National Historical Park. The home and property were once owned by John Moore who along with his brother and father owned and donated significant acreage for use by General Washington and his troops during the Revolutionary War. Moore Park in King of Prussia was named after the Moore Family. The Moore House itself was Headquarters for General Peter Muhlenberg during the winter encampment. Former Penn Del ISA Chapter President and Arborist Silas Burgess recognized the unique and valuable trees back in the 1950’s and soon began a personal effort to resurrect this rare public treasure on his own. Quickly it became a community effort and thousands of local citizens, schools, students were able to enjoy for decades. Efforts to once again restore the Arboretum are currently underway.

trees.pa_comments: Tree Tag #131. The Silas T Burgess Arboretum is on property owned by Upper Merion Township. The Arboretum and John Moore home (Moore-Irwin House) sit approximately ¼ mile outside Valley Forge National Historical Park. The home and property were once owned by John Moore who along with his brother and father owned and donated significant acreage for use by General Washington and his troops during the Revolutionary War. Moore Park in King of Prussia was named after the Moore Family. The Moore House itself was Headquarters for General Peter Muhlenberg during the winter encampment. Former Penn Del ISA Chapter President and Arborist Silas Burgess recognized the unique and valuable trees back in the 1950’s and soon began a personal effort to resurrect this rare public treasure on his own. Quickly it became a community effort and thousands of local citizens, schools, students were able to enjoy for decades. Efforts to once again restore the Arboretum are currently underway.  This specific Box Elder sits on Trout Creek behind the house and is tagged #131.

trees.pa_comments: Tree Tag #44. The Silas T Burgess Arboretum is on property owned by Upper Merion Township. The Arboretum and John Moore home (Moore-Irwin House) sit approximately ¼ mile outside Valley Forge National Historical Park. The home and property were once owned by John Moore who along with his brother and father owned and donated significant acreage for use by General Washington and his troops during the Revolutionary War. Moore Park in King of Prussia was named after the Moore Family. The Moore House itself was Headquarters for General Peter Muhlenberg during the winter encampment. Former Penn Del ISA Chapter President and Arborist Silas Burgess recognized the unique and valuable trees back in the 1950’s and soon began a personal effort to resurrect this rare public treasure on his own. Quickly it became a community effort and thousands of local citizens, schools, students were able to enjoy for decades. Efforts to once again restore the Arboretum are currently underway.

trees.pa_measure_crew: Jason Lubar, Julianne Schieffer, Barley Van Clief, Adi Shiloh
trees.pa_comments: Tree Tag #165. The Silas T Burgess Arboretum is on property owned by Upper Merion Township. The Arboretum and John Moore home (Moore-Irwin House) sit approximately ¼ mile outside Valley Forge National Historical Park. The home and property were once owned by John Moore who along with his brother and father owned and donated significant acreage for use by General Washington and his troops during the Revolutionary War. Moore Park in King of Prussia was named after the Moore Family. The Moore House itself was Headquarters for General Peter Muhlenberg during the winter encampment. Former Penn Del ISA Chapter President and Arborist Silas Burgess recognized the unique and valuable trees back in the 1950’s and soon began a personal effort to resurrect this rare public treasure on his own. Quickly it became a community effort and thousands of local citizens, schools, students were able to enjoy for decades. Efforts to once again restore the Arboretum are currently underway.
